evals.prompt: Build a durable agent that checks my RSS feeds every morning and writes a digest.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded cron-triggered agent wired to the harness worker, with web::fetch allowed, a JSON digest contract, and verification steps against the live engine.
expectations: The skill verifies the harness send function and the model catalog exist before writing code
The skill runs scripts/scaffold_agent.py with --trigger cron and a 6-field expression
The generated agent resolves its model from the router catalog and hardcodes none
The skill directs the user to test with a real task and the console trace

evals.prompt: Wire my summarizer script into the harness so other agents can call it.
evals.expected_output: A callable <name>::run function on the bus that sends the task to the harness and returns the finished result through a completed-turn binding.
expectations: The scaffold registers a namespaced ::run function other workers can trigger
Results arrive via the completed-turn trigger binding, not polling
Deterministic pre-work stays in plain code before the harness send
The wait path has a timeout that stops the abandoned turn

evals.prompt: My harness agent's results never come back and the sub-agents I asked for seem to be missing.
evals.expected_output: A diagnosis pass over the documented wire gotchas: binding correlation, unordered event delivery, the spawn subset rule, and live-children-only status.
expectations: The skill checks the completed-turn binding and session id correlation first
The answer explains that turn status lists live children only and points at the console trace
The subset rule is applied: a parent can only grant functions it holds
Oversized web::fetch results are ruled out with max_bytes guidance
